sanskrit:
यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत्।
तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते॥
transliteration:
yadā sattve pravṛddhe tu pralayaṃ yāti deha-bhṛt
tadottama-vidāṃ lokān amalān pratipadyate
literal_meaning: When / the mode of goodness / is predominant / indeed / dissolution / undergoes / the embodied one / then / of the highest knowers / worlds / pure / he attains
contextual_meaning: The mind that habitually operates from clarity, compassion, and self-reflection naturally migrates toward environments—inner or outer—that mirror and reinforce those qualities at the moment of transition (death of identity, relationship, career, or physical body).
psychological_state: A stable, integrated self-schema dominated by benevolent values; low cognitive dissonance; high moral identity; approaching major life transition with acceptance rather than dread.
trigger_scenario: A hospice patient who spent decades volunteering, forgiving old grievances, and practicing mindfulness greets death with calm anticipation, sensing an ‘inner light’ rather than terror.
anchor_text: Live in clarity; your final journey leads to luminous realms.
